notes: Lesson 17 Part 1 (act 99), 'Fixed-size window', 117.31s, 6 short cues. Concrete fixed-size walkthrough over the array [4,2,7,1,9] with window k=3: the first window is the only full addition (4+2+7=13), then every slide is a constant edit (drop the element that leaves, add the one that enters): 13-4+1=10, then 10-2+9=17 (a new best). Overlap (2,7) is never re-added; constant O(1) per step regardless of width; one full sum + a constant edit each remaining position = O(n) total. Uses the new arrays.py idiom lib (value_row, window_bracket, value_badge, edit_note, complexity) — the array/pointer vocabulary, MathTex reserved for the O(1)/O(n) bounds. One review iteration: authored, smoke-rendered every cue final frame with manim -s (fixed 2 safe-area overflows in Cue02/Cue03 by moving the sum/best badges to the right side and stacking the bottom text), then full -ql render (6 chunks, 854x480x15, niced/serial) + READ start/mid/end for all 6 cues; all pass with no further defects. cue05_end re-extracted @116.9s (near-EOF keyframe seek). Per-cue checklist (label legibility+math correctness, highlighted element matches the beat, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping via theme.guard, chunk timing within pinning tolerance): all pass. Final -ql 117.201s vs audio 117.31s (delta 0.109s, within tolerance).
